# mock

```go
import "github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/zenon/mock"
```

Package mock provides a test harness that builds an in\-memory Zenon node suitable for unit and integration tests.

### Overview

mock wires a fully working but ephemeral chain, consensus, and VM supervisor against an in\-memory leveldb plus the embedded [github.com/zenon\\\-network/go\\\-zenon/chain/genesis/mock](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/chain/genesis/mock/>) genesis. Tests use the [MockZenon](<#MockZenon>) interface to drive scenarios end to end — issue send blocks, force receives, advance momentums — without standing up a real network or pinning real time.

### Key Capabilities

Beyond the standard [zenon.Zenon](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/zenon/#Zenon>) surface, [MockZenon](<#MockZenon>) exposes:

- \[MockZenon.InsertNewMomentum\] / \[MockZenon.InsertMomentumsTo\] — drive consensus forward by one momentum \(or up to a target height\) without waiting for wall\-clock time. Time is virtualised via \[mockClock\], which sources its "now" from the frontier momentum's timestamp.
- \[MockZenon.CallContract\] — submit an embedded\-contract send and verify the contract's receive\-side outcome via the late caller pattern \(the assertion fires after the next InsertNewMomentum cements the receive\).
- \[MockZenon.InsertSendBlock\] / \[MockZenon.InsertReceiveBlock\] — finer\-grained block insertion with expected\-error and expected\-VM\-changes assertions.
- \[MockZenon.ExpectBalance\] / \[MockZenon.SaveLogs\] — common assertions used in embedded\-contract tests.

Producer logs from the pillar manager are intercepted by [ProducerLogSaver](<#ProducerLogSaver>); embedded\-block error strings are looked up by send\-block hash so \[MockZenon.CallContract\] can attribute them to the originating test step \([MockContractCaller](<#MockContractCaller>)\).

### Limitations

Verifier, Protocol, Producer, and Config accessors return nil — tests that need those subsystems should use a real Zenon instead. Broadcaster is wired to the mock itself so that block\-publishing flows \(e.g. the RPC PublishRawTransaction path\) round\-trip through CreateAccountBlock / CreateMomentum.

### Generated Files

None. Files are Zenon\-specific \(no upstream header\).

### Related Packages

- [github.com/zenon\\\-network/go\\\-zenon/zenon](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/zenon/>) — production orchestrator; [MockZenon](<#MockZenon>) satisfies its \[Zenon\] interface.
- [github.com/zenon\\\-network/go\\\-zenon/chain/genesis/mock](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/chain/genesis/mock/>) — keypair set and embedded genesis used by the harness.
- [github.com/zenon\\\-network/go\\\-zenon/vm/embedded/tests](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/vm/embedded/tests/>) — primary consumer of this package.

## Constants

<a name="SkipVmChanges"></a>VM\-change assertion sentinels for \[MockZenon.InsertSendBlock\] / \[MockZenon.InsertReceiveBlock\].

```go
const (
    // SkipVmChanges disables the changes-string assertion entirely.
    SkipVmChanges = "PASS-VM-CHANGES"
    // NoVmChanges asserts an empty change set (storage and balance
    // untouched) — the typical expectation for a no-op receive.
    NoVmChanges = `
storage
balance`
)
```

## Variables

<a name="AllLoggers"></a>AllLoggers is the full set of named loggers the harness silences / re\-routes during a test run. Saved\+restored around Stop so suites that share global state see consistent log handlers.

```go
var AllLoggers = []common.Logger{
    common.ZenonLogger,
    common.ChainLogger,
    common.SupervisorLogger,
    common.P2PLogger,
    common.PillarLogger,
    common.RPCLogger,
    common.WalletLogger,
    common.EmbeddedLogger,
    common.VmLogger,
    common.ProtocolLogger,
    common.FetcherLogger,
    common.DownloaderLogger,
}
```

<a name="getSignFunc"></a>
## func [getSignFunc](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L124>)

```go
func getSignFunc(address types.Address) vm.SignFunc
```

getSignFunc resolves a signer for address from the embedded genesis keypair set. Panics for unknown addresses since tests should only sign with known mock keys.

<a name="AutogeneratedBlockEntry"></a>
## type [AutogeneratedBlockEntry](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L64-L69>)

AutogeneratedBlockEntry is the JSON shape the pillar manager emits when it generates an embedded\-contract receive block. Decoded by [ProducerLogSaver](<#ProducerLogSaver>) so [MockContractCaller](<#MockContractCaller>) can later surface the contract's returned error to the originating test step.

```go
type AutogeneratedBlockEntry struct {
    Msg           string              `json:"msg"`
    Identifier    types.AccountHeader `json:"identifier"`
    SendBlock     types.AccountHeader `json:"send-block-header"`
    ReturnedError string              `json:"returned-error"`
}
```

<a name="MockContractCaller"></a>
## type [MockContractCaller](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L101-L106>)

MockContractCaller is the late\-caller used by \[MockZenon.CallContract\] — defers the contract's returned\-error assertion until after the next \[MockZenon.InsertNewMomentum\] cements the receive.

```go
type MockContractCaller struct {
    stack         string
    t             common.T
    sendBlockHash types.Hash
    results       *map[types.Hash]string
}
```

<a name="MockContractCaller.LateCallFunction"></a>
### func \(\*MockContractCaller\) [LateCallFunction](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L112>)

```go
func (mcc *MockContractCaller) LateCallFunction() (string, error)
```

LateCallFunction is invoked by the [common.Expecter](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/common/#Expecter>) after the receive block is cemented. Returns the contract's recorded error string; if the send hasn't been receipted yet, returns a hint that the test forgot to call InsertNewMomentum.

<a name="MockZenon"></a>
## type [MockZenon](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/interfaces.go#L14-L27>)

MockZenon is the test\-harness facade. Extends [zenon.Zenon](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/zenon/#Zenon>) with time\-stepping \(Insert\* momentum helpers\), block\-insertion helpers with expected\-error / expected\-VM\-changes assertions, contract call helpers, and per\-test cleanup \(StopPanic\).

```go
type MockZenon interface {
    zenon.Zenon
    StopPanic()

    InsertNewMomentum()
    InsertMomentumsTo(targetHeight uint64)

    CallContract(template *nom.AccountBlock) *common.Expecter
    InsertSendBlock(template *nom.AccountBlock, expectedError error, expectedVmChanges string) *nom.AccountBlock
    InsertReceiveBlock(fromHeader types.AccountHeader, template *nom.AccountBlock, expectedError error, expectedVmChanges string) *nom.AccountBlock

    SaveLogs(logger common.Logger) *common.Expecter
    ExpectBalance(address types.Address, standard types.ZenonTokenStandard, expected int64)
}
```

<a name="NewMockZenon"></a>
### func [NewMockZenon](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L460>)

```go
func NewMockZenon(t common.T) MockZenon
```

NewMockZenon builds a fresh in\-memory test harness using the default consensus EpochDuration. Caller is responsible for invoking \[MockZenon.StopPanic\] \(typically via t.Cleanup\) so log handlers are restored.

<a name="NewMockZenonWithCustomEpochDuration"></a>
### func [NewMockZenonWithCustomEpochDuration](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L467>)

```go
func NewMockZenonWithCustomEpochDuration(t common.T, epochDuration time.Duration) MockZenon
```

NewMockZenonWithCustomEpochDuration builds a harness with a non\-default epoch duration — useful for testing epoch\-rollover edge cases without waiting for the full default duration.

<a name="newMockZenon"></a>
### func [newMockZenon](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L476>)

```go
func newMockZenon(t common.T, customEpochDuration time.Duration) MockZenon
```

newMockZenon is the shared constructor: silences chain / consensus / supervisor loggers, opens an in\-memory chain on t.TempDir\(\), seats every genesis pillar key as its own pillar, installs the \[mockClock\], and returns a started harness ready for use.

<a name="ProducerLogSaver"></a>
## type [ProducerLogSaver](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L75-L79>)

ProducerLogSaver is a log15 Format that intercepts pillar "generated embedded\-block" entries and stores the \(sendBlockHash → returnedError\) mapping in results. [MockContractCaller](<#MockContractCaller>) queries this map.

```go
type ProducerLogSaver struct {
    format  log15.Format
    buffer  *bytes.Buffer
    results map[types.Hash]string
}
```

<a name="ProducerLogSaver.Format"></a>
### func \(\*ProducerLogSaver\) [Format](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L84>)

```go
func (f *ProducerLogSaver) Format(r *log15.Record) []byte
```

Format renders r via the embedded JSON formatter and, if the record describes a generated embedded block, indexes its returned\-error string by send\-block hash.

<a name="mockClock"></a>
## type [mockClock](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L136-L139>)

mockClock virtualises [common.Clock](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/common/#Clock>). "Now" tracks the frontier momentum's timestamp so tests step time forward by inserting momentums rather than relying on wall\-clock progression.

```go
type mockClock struct {
    chain    chain.Chain
    lastTime *time.Time
}
```

<a name="mockClock.Now"></a>
### func \(\*mockClock\) [Now](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L143>)

```go
func (clock *mockClock) Now() time.Time
```

Now returns the frontier momentum's timestamp, falling back to the last cached value once the chain is shut down.

<a name="mockZenon"></a>
## type [mockZenon](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L159-L173>)

mockZenon is the production [MockZenon](<#MockZenon>) implementation. Contains one chain \+ consensus \+ supervisor stack plus a slice of pillars \(one per genesis pillar key\) so any required momentum producer can be selected per\-tick.

```go
type mockZenon struct {
    lastTime         *time.Time
    t                common.T
    log              log15.Logger
    producerLogSaver *ProducerLogSaver

    pillars    []pillar.Manager
    chain      chain.Chain
    consensus  consensus.Consensus
    supervisor *vm.Supervisor

    loggers              []log15.Logger
    handlers             []log15.Handler
    initialEpochDuration time.Duration
}
```

<a name="mockZenon.Broadcaster"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [Broadcaster](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L452>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) Broadcaster() protocol.Broadcaster
```



<a name="mockZenon.CallContract"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [CallContract](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L268>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) CallContract(template *nom.AccountBlock) *common.Expecter
```

CallContract is the canonical embedded\-contract test pattern: inserts template as a UserSend block \(skipping VM\-changes assertion since contract receives are async\), then returns a late\-caller [common.Expecter](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/common/#Expecter>) that surfaces the contract's returned error once the receive is cemented by the next InsertNewMomentum.

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) Chain() chain.Chain
```

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) Config() *zenon.Config
```

<a name="mockZenon.CreateAccountBlock"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [CreateAccountBlock](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L210>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) CreateAccountBlock(accountBlockTransaction *nom.AccountBlockTransaction)
```

CreateAccountBlock is the [protocol.Broadcaster](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/protocol/#Broadcaster>) hook for newly produced account blocks. Logs \(does not panic on\) insert failures so tests can see VM\-validation rejections directly.

<a name="mockZenon.CreateMomentum"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [CreateMomentum](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L195>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) CreateMomentum(momentumTransaction *nom.MomentumTransaction)
```

CreateMomentum is the [protocol.Broadcaster](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/protocol/#Broadcaster>) hook for the pillar manager: pillars produce momentums and call this to commit them. Panics on insert failure — the mock always operates against a valid chain.

<a name="mockZenon.EmbeddedContext"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [EmbeddedContext](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L350>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) EmbeddedContext(address types.Address) vm_context.AccountVmContext
```

EmbeddedContext returns a read\-only VM context bound to address's frontier state — useful for inspecting embedded\-contract storage directly from tests.

<a name="mockZenon.ExpectBalance"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [ExpectBalance](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L370>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) ExpectBalance(address types.Address, standard types.ZenonTokenStandard, expected int64)
```

ExpectBalance asserts that address's balance for standard equals expected. Treats nil \(uninitialised\) balances as 0.

<a name="mockZenon.Init"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [Init](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L384>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) Init() error
```

Init initialises the chain, consensus, and every pillar. Subsystems not modeled by the mock \(verifier, protocol, subscribe\) are skipped.

<a name="mockZenon.InsertMomentumsTo"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [InsertMomentumsTo](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L255>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) InsertMomentumsTo(targetHeight uint64)
```

InsertMomentumsTo repeatedly calls InsertNewMomentum until the chain head reaches targetHeight. No\-op if already at or past the target.

<a name="mockZenon.InsertNewMomentum"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [InsertNewMomentum](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L229>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) InsertNewMomentum()
```

InsertNewMomentum advances the chain by exactly one momentum: computes the next slot's expected producer via the consensus election, looks up the matching pillar by coinbase, and waits for it to mint\+commit. Panics if the consensus returns no producer for the slot.

<a name="mockZenon.InsertReceiveBlock"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [InsertReceiveBlock](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L309>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) InsertReceiveBlock(fromHeader types.AccountHeader, template *nom.AccountBlock, expectedError error, expectedVmChanges string) *nom.AccountBlock
```

InsertReceiveBlock issues a receive block matching the cemented send identified by fromHeader. Auto\-fills BlockType / FromBlockHash / Address from the source send when unset on template, then asserts expectedError / expectedVmChanges as in InsertSendBlock.

<a name="mockZenon.InsertSendBlock"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [InsertSendBlock](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L290>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) InsertSendBlock(template *nom.AccountBlock, expectedError error, expectedVmChanges string) *nom.AccountBlock
```

InsertSendBlock issues a UserSend block from template, asserting expectedError matches the supervisor's verdict. On success, optionally asserts the produced patch matches expectedVmChanges \(use [SkipVmChanges](<#SkipVmChanges>) to skip the changes assertion\).

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) Producer() pillar.Manager
```

<a name="mockZenon.SaveLogs"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [SaveLogs](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L364>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) SaveLogs(logger common.Logger) *common.Expecter
```

SaveLogs starts capturing logger output for later assertion via the returned [common.Expecter](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/common/#Expecter>).

<a name="mockZenon.Start"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [Start](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L395>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) Start() error
```

Start launches each modelled subsystem; pillars are started last so they do not fire before the chain accepts inserts.

<a name="mockZenon.Stop"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [Stop](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L408>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) Stop() error
```

Stop tears down pillars / consensus / chain, restores log handlers, and resets the global EpochDuration so a subsequent test starts from a clean slate. Panics on errors — Stop should never fail in test scenarios.

<a name="mockZenon.StopPanic"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [StopPanic](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L429>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) StopPanic()
```

StopPanic is the typical t.Cleanup target — calls Stop and panics on error. Named to make defer'd usage obvious.

<a name="mockZenon.SyncInfo"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [SyncInfo](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L178>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) SyncInfo() *protocol.SyncInfo
```

SyncInfo always reports SyncDone — the mock never simulates a catch\-up phase, so tests skip the gating that would otherwise suppress producer activity until sync completes.

<a name="mockZenon.SyncState"></a>
### func \(\*mockZenon\) [SyncState](<https://github.com/zenon-network/go-zenon/blob/master/zenon/mock/zenon.go#L187>)

```go
func (zenon *mockZenon) SyncState() protocol.SyncState
```

SyncState mirrors SyncInfo — always SyncDone.
